---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-07"
---

```{r}
library(crunch)
library(here)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
#install.packages("numerator")
#library(numerator)
library(nnet)
library(tidyverse)
library(purrr)
library(srvyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(DT)
library(lubridate)

# if needed for numerator package
 install.packages("remotes")
# remotes::install_github("cchoe/numerator")

# first time set up
 if (!require(" #usethis")) install.packages("usethis")
 usethis::edit_r_environ()
 R_CRUNCH_API="https://moreincommon.crunch.io/api/"
 R_CRUNCH_API_KEY="apk-SGjaXP9zHeIkEjB_dLQ7Mpj4ZY3rGEQqXuye57M-Ogk="

# Load datasets from BOTH the main UK folder (2025) and the 2024 Datasets subfolder

# Get 2025 datasets from main UK folder
ds_name_2025 <- listDatasets(kind="all", project="UK", refresh=TRUE)

# Get 2024 datasets from the subfolder
ds_name_2024 <- listDatasets(kind="all", project="UK/2024 Datasets", refresh=TRUE)

# Combine all datasets
ds_name <- c(ds_name_2024, ds_name_2025)

# Remove NNR datasets
ds_name <- ds_name[!grepl("NNR", ds_name)]

# Keep datasets from October 2024 to October 2025
months_2024 <- paste0("24-", c("10", "11", "12"))
months_2025 <- paste0("25-", c("01", "02", "03", "04", "05", "06", "07", "08", "09", "10"))
keep_dates <- paste(c(months_2024, months_2025), collapse="|")

ds_name <- ds_name[grep(keep_dates, ds_name)]

# Filter: keep all 2024 OR (2025 with WO)
ds_name <- ds_name[grepl("24-(10|11|12)", ds_name) | (grepl("^25-", ds_name) & grepl("WO", ds_name))]

# Remove -CX--Default-SPSS suffix
cx_ind <- grep("-CX--Default-SPSS", ds_name, fixed=TRUE)
if(length(cx_ind) > 0){
  ds_name <- ds_name[-cx_ind]
}

# Check results
print(paste("Total datasets:", length(ds_name)))
print(paste("2024 datasets:", sum(grepl("24-", ds_name))))
print(paste("2025 datasets:", sum(grepl("25-", ds_name))))

# Load the datasets
ds_list <- list()  
k <- 1
for(i in seq_along(ds_name)){
  # Determine which project to use based on dataset name
  if(grepl("^24-", ds_name[i])){
    project_path <- "UK/2024 Datasets"
  } else {
    project_path <- "UK"
  }
  
  tmp <- try(loadDataset(ds_name[i], project=project_path, refresh=TRUE))
  if(inherits(tmp, "try-error")){
    message(paste0(ds_name[i], " failed to load.\n"))
  } else {
    ds_list[[k]] <- tmp  
    names(ds_list)[k] <- ds_name[i] 
    k <- k+1
  }
}


 saveRDS(ds_list, "ds_list.rds")
  
 ds_list <- readRDS("ds_list.rds")
 
## This is the date extraction - it looks at the START_DATE variable INSIDE each dataset

for(i in seq_along(ds_list)){
  unlock(ds_list[[i]])
  
  # Unhide START_DATE if it's hidden
  if("START_DATE" %in% names(ds_list[[i]])){
    tryCatch({
      hiddenVariables(ds_list[[i]]) <- setdiff(hiddenVariables(ds_list[[i]]), "START_DATE")
    }, error = function(e) {
      message("Could not unhide START_DATE in dataset ", i)
    })
  }
  
# Now extract dates
  tryCatch({
    dates <- as.data.frame(as.vector(ds_list[[i]]$START_DATE))[,1]
    dates <- as.Date(substr(dates, 1, 10))
    start <- min(dates, na.rm = TRUE)
    end <- max(dates, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    startDate(ds_list[[i]]) <- start
    endDate(ds_list[[i]]) <- end
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error processing dates for dataset ", i, ": ", e$message)
    # Use dataset name to extract date as fallback
    date_from_name <- str_extract(names(ds_list)[i], "\\d{2}-\\d{2}")
    if(!is.na(date_from_name)){
      fallback_date <- dmy(paste0(date_from_name, "-2024"))
      startDate(ds_list[[i]]) <- fallback_date
      endDate(ds_list[[i]]) <- fallback_date
      message("  Used fallback date from dataset name: ", fallback_date)
    }
  })
}


ds_info <- data.frame(
  index = integer(),
  name = character(),
  start_date = as.Date(character()),
  end_date = as.Date(character()),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

null2na <- function(x)ifelse(is.null(x), NA, x)

for(i in seq_along(ds_list)){
  ds_info <- rbind(ds_info, data.frame(
    index = i,
    name = names(ds_list)[i],
    start_date = null2na(startDate(ds_list[[i]])),
    end_date = null2na(endDate(ds_list[[i]]))
  ))
}




ds_info <- ds_info %>% arrange(desc(end_date))

print(ds_info)

saveRDS(ds_info, "ds_info.rds")

 ds_info <- readRDS("ds_info.rds")


## Downloading all the data - this part will take 30-40  mins as it is downloading all the relevant datasets

df_list <- list()
for(i in seq_along(ds_list)){
  df_list[[i]] <- as.data.frame(ds_list[[i]], force=TRUE)
}

saveRDS(df_list, "df_list.rds")

  
 df_list <- readRDS("df_list.rds")
 

# Function to calculate voting intention by gender-age 
calculate_vote_by_gender_age <- function(df_list, ds_list) {
  
  voting_by_gender_age <- NULL
  ## Has to be to votingintention_headline
  ## making a vector of names for the voting and weight variables
  vv <- c("voting_intention_headline", "votingintention_headline")
  wv <- c("UKNatRepWeight", "GBnatrep", "uknatrep", "weightGB", 
          "uknatrepweight", "GBNatRepWeight")
  
  for (i in seq_along(df_list)) {
    ## remove dataset with very few observations in votingintention_headline
    if(names(ds_list)[[i]] == "24-11a - Movember, Assisted Dying")next
    # Determine which voting variable to use - check all possible variations
    # use intersect() with vote variable names to find the first matching element
    # it is a bit more compact than nested if-else statements. 
    vote_var <- intersect(names(df_list[[i]]), vv)[1]
    if(is.na(vote_var)){
      message(paste("No voting_intention_headline variable in dataset ", i, ": ", 
                    names(df_list)[i], " skipping dataset.\n", sep=""))
      next
    }
    # using intersect to find available weight variables, choosing the first one
    wtvar <- intersect(names(df_list[[i]]), wv)[1]
    if(is.na(wtvar)){
        message(paste("No weight variable in dataset ", i, ": ", 
                      names(df_list)[i], " skipping dataset.\n", sep=""))
        next  # Skip to next iteration
    }
      
      # Get the date and ensure it's a Date object
      dataset_date <- endDate(ds_list[[i]])
      if(is.character(dataset_date)) {
        dataset_date <- as.Date(dataset_date)
      }
      
      # Calculate weighted tallies
      voting_by_gender_age <- bind_rows(
        voting_by_gender_age,
        df_list[[i]] %>%
          filter(!is.na(!!sym(vote_var))) %>%  
          as_survey_design(weights = !!sym(wtvar)) %>%
          filter(age_1 >= 18 & age_1 <= 28) %>%
          group_by(gender, !!sym(vote_var)) %>%
          survey_tally() %>%
          mutate(
            ds_number = i, 
            dataset = names(df_list)[i],
            date = dataset_date,  # Use the pre-converted date
            weight = wtvar,
            vote_variable = vote_var
          )
      )
    }
  return(voting_by_gender_age)  
}

voting_results <- calculate_vote_by_gender_age(df_list, ds_list)

table(voting_results$date)
print(voting_results)



library(RcppRoll)
library(lubridate)
##voting_results %>% 
##group_by(gender, votingintention_headline) %>% 
##reframe(n = roll_sum(n, 3, align="right"))


voting_results_roll <- voting_results %>% 
  mutate(yearmon = gsub("(^\\d{2}-\\d{2}).*", "\\1", names(ds_list)[ds_number]), 
         yearmon = lubridate::ym(yearmon)) %>% 
  arrange(gender, votingintention_headline, date) %>%  # Make sure data is sorted by date
  group_by(gender, votingintention_headline) %>% 
  filter(n() >= 3) %>%  # Only calculate for groups with 3+ observations
  reframe(
    n = roll_sum(n, 3, align="right", fill = NA), 
    start_num = roll_min(ds_number, 3, align="right", fill=NA), 
    end_num = roll_max(ds_number, 3, align="right", fill=NA),
    start = roll_min(as.numeric(as.Date(date)), 3, align="right", fill = NA), 
    end = roll_max(as.numeric(as.Date(date)), 3, align="right", fill = NA)
  ) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(n)) %>%  # Remove rows where rolling sum couldn't be calculated
  mutate(
    start = as.Date(start, origin = "1970-01-01"),  # Convert back to Date
    end = as.Date(end, origin = "1970-01-01")       # Convert back to Date
  ) %>%
  group_by(gender, start) %>% 
  mutate(pct = n/sum(n) * 100, 
         yrmon1 = ym(sprintf("%s-%s", year(start), month(start))), 
         yrmon2 = ym(gsub("^(\\d{2}-\\d{2}).*", "\\1", names(ds_list)[[start_num[1]]])),
         yrmon = coalesce(yrmon1, yrmon2)) %>% 
  select(-c(yrmon1, yrmon2)) %>% 
  ungroup()

voting_results_roll_yrmon <- voting_results_roll %>% 
  group_by(gender, votingintention_headline, yrmon) %>% 
  summarise(pct = mean(pct))

ggplot(voting_results_roll_yrmon, 
       aes(x=yrmon, y=pct, color=gender)) + 
  geom_line() + 
  facet_wrap(~votingintention_headline) + 
  theme_bw()

print(voting_results_roll_yrmon)

write.csv(voting_results_roll_yrmon, "voting_results_roll_yrmon.csv")


## only surveys starting in ocotebr
voting_results_roll %>% 
  filter(start > as.Date("2024-10-01"))
  
## need to go back to 2024 octoebr - need to change - 278

# last two polls in sept and first poll in octoebr
##voting_results_roll %>% 
##  filter(end > as.Date("2025-10-01"))

```

labour_men_2 <-voting_results %>% filter(gender=="Male", votingintention_headline=="Labour")

labour_men_3 <- voting_results %>% filter(date == as.Date("2024-11-11"))


library(ggplot2)

# Filter for data starting from October 2024
voting_plot_data <- voting_results_roll %>%
  filter(start > as.Date("2024-10-01"))

# Create the plot
ggplot(voting_plot_data, aes(x = end, y = pct, color = votingintention_headline)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ gender, ncol = 1) +  # Separate panels for Male/Female
  labs(
    title = "Voting Intention by Gender (3-Week Rolling Average)",
    subtitle = "Young voters aged 18-28, October 2024 onwards",
    x = "Date (End of Rolling Period)",
    y = "Percentage (%)",
    color = "Party"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%b %Y") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  )

# Save the plot
ggsave("voting_intention_by_gender.png", 
       width = 12, 
       height = 8, 
       dpi = 300)



library(ggplot2)

# Filter for data starting from October 2024 and exclude certain parties
voting_results_roll_yrmon <- voting_results_roll_yrmon %>%
# filter(start > as.Date("2024-10-01")) %>%
  filter(!votingintention_headline %in% c("Scottish National Party (SNP)", 
                                          "Plaid Cymru", 
                                          "Another party",
                                          "Another party/Independent candidate",
                                          "Another party/Independent Candidate"))

# Define UK party colors (official hex codes)
party_colors <- c(
  "Conservative" = "#0087DC",      # Conservative Blue
  "Labour" = "#E4003B",            # Labour Red
  "Liberal Democrat" = "#FAA61A",   # Lib Dem Orange/Yellow
  "The Green Party" = "#6AB023",   # Green
  "Reform UK" = "#12B6CF"          # Reform Turquoise
)

# Create the plot
ggplot(voting_results_roll_yrmon, aes(x = yrmon, y = pct, color = votingintention_headline)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ gender, ncol = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = party_colors) +  # Use official party colors
  labs(
    title = "Voting Intention by Gender (3-Week Rolling Average)",
    subtitle = "Young voters aged 18-28, October 2024 onwards",
    x = "Date (End of Rolling Period)",
    y = "Percentage (%)",
    color = "Party"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%b %Y") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA), breaks = seq(0, 60, by = 10)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )



labour_men <- voting_plot_data %>% filter(gender =="Male" & votingintention_headline =="Labour")


# Save the plot
ggsave("voting_intention_by_gender.png", 
       width = 12, 
       height = 8, 
       dpi = 300)
